channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-13526] Client eviction mitigation
item.description:
We have a user that writes/read 52000 restart files using 52000 cores on 1300 nodes. During write cycle a several clients were evicted. We would like to know if this is expected. What can we do to mitigate client evictions or manage client load.

Filesystem background:

OST# = 359

OSS# = 20

Filesystem Size= 18P

Free space = %5

 Apr 29 14:36:12 nbp2-oss11 rsyslogd: -- MARK --
Apr 29 14:39:27 nbp2-oss11 kernel: [13220183.158983] Lustre: 17955:0:(service.c:1372:ptlrpc_at_send_early_reply()) @@@ Couldn't add any time (30/30), not sending early reply
Apr 29 14:39:27 nbp2-oss11 kernel: [13220183.158983]   req@ffff91a2c9552050 x1664344156868944/t0(0) o4->fd329a67-7aa9-5ce6-277a-db7f93ba95aa@10.149.13.21@o2ib313:292/0 lens 488/448 e 0 to 0 dl 1588196397 ref 2 fl Interpret:/0/0 rc 0/0
Apr 29 14:39:27 nbp2-oss11 kernel: [13220183.247695] Lustre: 17955:0:(service.c:1372:ptlrpc_at_send_early_reply()) Skipped 34664 previous similar messages
Apr 29 14:40:28 nbp2-oss11 kernel: [13220244.019145] LustreError: 17999:0:(ldlm_lib.c:3243:target_bulk_io()) @@@ timeout on bulk WRITE after 0+0s  req@ffff91a64368a850 x1664344138126592/t0(0) o4->b563eaec-b2d0-3747-168c-69ed81119736@10.149.6.81@o2ib313:323/0 lens 488/448 e 0 to 0 dl 1588196428 ref 1 fl Interpret:/0/0 rc 0/0

Apr 29 15:11:40 nbp2-oss11 kernel: [13222116.235086] Lustre: nbp2-OST015e: Connection restored to 65ec87b7-8769-2c1a-a9b0-6a06a50f62a9 (at 10.151.5.38@o2ib)
Apr 29 15:11:40 nbp2-oss11 kernel: [13222116.235091] Lustre: Skipped 4232 previous similar messages

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
yes, sorry for the delay just as we discussed, the patches are from LU-13131: 37468, 37918, 37992, 38114 and 38292

there's going to be another patch for some further pathological cases, but the 5 above should be good to have for now.
Oleg as per the discussion on the call yesterday, I understand that you are going to point to some patches that might be of interest to NASA

All of your messages point at a severely overloaded disk system that takes way longer than expected to process.

You can try increasing obd_timeout and at_max cluster-wise to see if that would help nodes to be more patient but might also lead to just waiting longer and timing out anyway. I see you are hitting at_max that's default at 600 seconds normally.

I think you just need to lower the load on the backend. Ideally just spreading the load over more OSTs/spindles if you have any more, other than that reducing the number of io threads on OSS nodes might also help.
